reasoning_model_1: Model 1 exhibits a critical content accuracy error. The script requires the sentence to end with 'okay?', but the generated audio abruptly stops after 'later,' omitting the final word. This truncation severely impacts the paralinguistic performance. For the 'lively' dimension, the sudden cut-off at the end of the sentence destroys the energetic flow and leaves the prosody unresolved. For the 'mischievous' dimension, the omission of the tag question 'okay?' removes the primary vehicle for the teasing, playful tone intended at the conclusion of the script. Consequently, the target emotions are incompletely and poorly expressed.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 faithfully reproduces the full text script, including the final 'okay?', maintaining perfect content accuracy and natural fluency. In terms of the 'lively' dimension, the model sustains an energetic pace throughout the sentence, particularly in the opening exclamation 'Hey, check out...'. Regarding the 'mischievous' dimension, the model effectively delivers the conditional warning 'Don't cry if your snacks disappear later' with a playful tone, properly utilizing the final 'okay?' to seal the teasing intent with the correct intonation.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 suffers from a major content failure (omission of the final word), which renders the audio unnatural and prevents the full realization of the requested emotional tones. The abrupt ending in Model 1 kills the 'lively' momentum and deletes the 'mischievous' tag question. Model 2 provides a complete, accurate, and stylistically appropriate generation that satisfies all prompt requirements.
